name: Sutton Scotney
overview: Sutton Scotney is a village in the civil parish of Wonston, in Hampshire, lying alongside the River Dever about 7 miles north of Winchester. With a population of around 1,346, it is now bypassed by the A34 trunk road, which gives quick links north to Newbury and south to Winchester and Southampton. The village retains a good range of everyday amenities and a large recreation ground.
character: A practical, well-served Dever-valley village with a strong commuter appeal thanks to fast A34 access, while keeping its rural setting.
amenities: The Coach and Horses pub (with B&B)
Large recreation ground (The Gratton) with children's play park and football pitches
River Dever and trout fisheries nearby

schools.name: Wonston Primary / South Wonston Primary (nearest village primaries)
Secondary schools in Winchester (e.g. Henry Beaufort, Kings')

prices.summary: A mix of period cottages, modern detached family homes and some social housing; popular with commuters for its A34 access. See Rightmove for current sold-price detail.

thingsToDo: Trout fishing on the River Dever (Dever Springs/Sutton Springs)
Walks in the Dever valley and Hampshire Downs
Easy trips to Winchester and Highclere Castle
pros: Excellent A34 commuter access (Winchester, Newbury, Southampton)
Good everyday amenities: shop, surgery, pub, garage
Large recreation ground and riverside setting
Bypassed by the A34, so quieter village core
cons: No primary school in the village itself (nearby villages provide one)
No railway station; rely on Winchester
Proximity to the A34 brings some traffic noise nearby
whoItSuits: Commuters and families wanting an amenity-rich village with fast road links north and south of Winchester.
